examples: Does he call you at whacky times?
Ideas for reducing fossil fuel use by business vary from pedestrian to whacky.
I hear that Mel Gibson allegedly blamed what he called depressed and "whacky" behavior on "male menopause."
Conspiracy theorists this morning received fresh fodder from another set of whacky employment figures.
But the business secretary said some of the proposals in the paper were "a bit whacky".
